replies.msg:
Not a practising barrister but as a law graduate I thought I’d offer my two cents.

In your legal studies, you won’t be covering EVERYTHING in law - there certainly isn’t enough time to do so. You cover in your studies the main principles of each area of law, the main cases. When you practise, you’ll certainly face areas of law you’ve never learnt in school (especially if it’s really niche area of the law) - and thats why you will always have to do legal research. Mostly on online database (Lexis, Westlaw), where you find precedents (previous cases) covering those principles or similar facts. Just like you say, cases update, and there might always be new cases. So even if it’s areas of law you’ve covered in your legal studies, you’ll still have to do legal research to see if that’s more updated rulings, or updated legislations.

I think the biggest thing one learns in law school thus is not ‘the law’, ‘厚厚的法律條文’ as some would think, but rather, how to research the law, how to interpret the law, and how to apply the law.

其實一單case 入面要做legal research係咪代表即係要搵案例？
D 案例邊度搵？係高院嗰圖書館搵定點？係咪全部讀書學到？有d新案例出咗你點知？

Not a practising barrister but as a law graduate I thought I’d offer my two cents.

In your legal studies, you won’t be covering EVERYTHING in law - there certainly isn’t enough time to do so. You cover in your studies the main principles of each area of law, the main cases. When you practise, you’ll certainly face areas of law you’ve never learnt in school (especially if it’s really niche area of the law) - and thats why you will always have to do legal research. Mostly on online database (Lexis, Westlaw), where you find precedents (previous cases) covering those principles or similar facts. Just like you say, cases update, and there might always be new cases. So even if it’s areas of law you’ve covered in your legal studies, you’ll still have to do legal research to see if that’s more updated rulings, or updated legislations.

I think the biggest thing one learns in law school thus is not ‘the law’, ‘厚厚的法律條文’ as some would think, but rather, how to research the law, how to interpret the law, and how to apply the law.

如果係啲好個人嘅事情建議你tg搵翻樓主或者相熟嘅律師朋友
其他人係到唔會覆到你咁多（包括樓主）

我可以同你講 樓主講嘅 唔會有法律責任係可以apply落好多common law 嘅jurisdictions 但係並唔絕對適用係香港
事關香港係呢方面 對「施救者」嘅保障係少之又少 前幾年嘅時候嘈過一輪要本地立法 上過幾次立會 但暫時都係未見咩風聲（香港嘅法律改革一向好保守）

我俾你嘅答案係 大多數情況下唔會，但要有心理準備 家屬可以就你嘅行為去索償或者報狗做刑事嘅investigation 然後決定控告你嘅話（通常都係manslaughter）法官會根據翻普通法嘅原則去審你

係咪免責其實有好多factors要考慮 例如傷者有冇即時嘅生命危險，你又冇嘗試call999make reasonable attempt（如果情況許可），你施救嘅過程有冇act reasonably 係咪魯莽 嚴重疏忽等等

同埋係連登呢度俾嘅最多係法律資訊 絕對唔可以當法律意見（包括我上面講嘅）
我唸樓主都分身不暇做唔到每個問題都詳細覆 但係咁樣對其他巴打絲打好危險 所以如果可以戴翻個頭盔加句disclaimer可能會好啲

I’ve done some minimal research into LLM in Germany - from my understanding, most LLMs in Germany are conducted in German, unless for those focused on ‘international business law’, ‘international tax’, international dispute resolution etc. For these, the courses will be conducted in English.
So in that sense, if you want a master in private international law and think perspective in this area will help, then the LLM should help, and whether Germany is under the continental Law system (or we call civil law) should not really cause a worry, since after all all the LLMs in English in Germany will be on international law anyway.

However I have never heard of anyone in HK with a law degree from Germany - so I don’t know how recognised it will be. This is despite the fact that Germany has a lot of top quality law schools.
Be wary of the fact that I think they do require a law degree to study for an LLM in Germany.

quote.msg: 冇first aid牌但係識first aid 救人但係救唔翻，會唔會有法律責任？

如果係啲好個人嘅事情建議你tg搵翻樓主或者相熟嘅律師朋友
其他人係到唔會覆到你咁多（包括樓主）

我可以同你講 樓主講嘅 唔會有法律責任係可以apply落好多common law 嘅jurisdictions 但係並唔絕對適用係香港
事關香港係呢方面 對「施救者」嘅保障係少之又少 前幾年嘅時候嘈過一輪要本地立法 上過幾次立會 但暫時都係未見咩風聲（香港嘅法律改革一向好保守）

我俾你嘅答案係 大多數情況下唔會，但要有心理準備 家屬可以就你嘅行為去索償或者報狗做刑事嘅investigation 然後決定控告你嘅話（通常都係manslaughter）法官會根據翻普通法嘅原則去審你

係咪免責其實有好多factors要考慮 例如傷者有冇即時嘅生命危險，你又冇嘗試call999make reasonable attempt（如果情況許可），你施救嘅過程有冇act reasonably 係咪魯莽 嚴重疏忽等等

同埋係連登呢度俾嘅最多係法律資訊 絕對唔可以當法律意見（包括我上面講嘅）
我唸樓主都分身不暇做唔到每個問題都詳細覆 但係咁樣對其他巴打絲打好危險 所以如果可以戴翻個頭盔加句disclaimer可能會好啲

我可以同你講 樓主講嘅 唔會有法律責任係可以apply落好多common law 嘅jurisdictions 但係並唔絕對適用係香港
事關香港係呢方面 對「施救者」嘅保障係少之又少 前幾年嘅時候嘈過一輪要本地立法 上過幾次立會 但暫時都係未見咩風聲（香港嘅法律改革一向好保守）

我俾你嘅答案係 大多數情況下唔會，但要有心理準備 家屬可以就你嘅行為去索償或者報狗做刑事嘅investigation 然後決定控告你嘅話（通常都係manslaughter）法官會根據翻普通法嘅原則去審你

係咪免責其實有好多factors要考慮 例如傷者有冇即時嘅生命危險，你又冇嘗試call999make reasonable attempt（如果情況許可），你施救嘅過程有冇act reasonably 係咪魯莽 嚴重疏忽等等

同埋係連登呢度俾嘅最多係法律資訊 絕對唔可以當法律意見（包括我上面講嘅）
我唸樓主都分身不暇做唔到每個問題都詳細覆 但係咁樣對其他巴打絲打好危險 所以如果可以戴翻個頭盔加句disclaimer可能會好啲
